title: Unicode, Language, Tokenization, Fields, and Citation Offsets · Program Brief: Rag Language Tokenization
summary: A search service lowercases and compatibility-normalizes every field, tokenizes all languages on spaces, removes legal negation as a stop word, and can no longer link a hit to its source span. Predict the stakeholder decision, corpus and query identity, relevance assumption, failure cost, and evidence that would falsify the proposed RAG claim before opening the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the language tokenization increment in reversible multilingual text pipeline. Learne…
objectives: Detect or receive language and apply language- and script-appropriate word, subword, character, or morphological tokenization.
Choose and version Unicode normalization per field while retaining original text and testing canonical and compatibility distinctions.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""rag-retrieval-augmented-generation cumulative evidence-grounded retrieval portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python models and fixed fixtures only.
Native ML packages, files, models, vector stores, provider APIs, networks, credentials,
OCR, load, faults, recovery systems, and production effects require authorized transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RagEvidence:
    corpus_version: str
    document_id: str
    chunk_id: str
    query_id: str
    source_span: str


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RetrievalBoundary:
    tenant_id: str
    authorized: bool
    answerable: bool
    context_budget: int


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python RAG engineering portfolio ready"

Build deterministic Python evidence to normalize and tokenize multilingual fields without losing source text or citation offsets; measure retrieval and generation separately before accepting an end-to-end score; then move the only supporting passage to the middle of the context and rerun the position slice. Browser code uses pure bounded models and fixed fixtures only. A defensible solution applies Rag Language Tokenization, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to reversible multilingual text pipeline.

content.code:
# Evidence: rag-worked-rag-normalization-tokenization-4
# Competency: rag-worked-rag-normalization-tokenization-4.
# Case 1655: an incident-response knowledge search.
# Operating constraint: measure retrieval and generation separately before accepting an end-to-end score.
# Changed case: double corpus size and candidate depth while preserving the latency, memory, and cost envelope.
def worked_rag_normalization_tokenization_4(original: str = "Café API v2", normalized: str = "café api v2", language: str = "fr", field_count: int = 3, offsets_preserved: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked4 = "worked4-4dekrow-1655"
    assert evidence_variant_worked4
    evidence_axes_worked4 = (
        "corpusworked4",
        "query4dekrow",
        "tenant1655",
        "indexworked41655",
        "prompt4dekrow1655",
        "evaluation1655worked4",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked4)) == 6
    if not original or not normalized:
        return False, 0, "empty-text"
    if not language or field_count < 1:
        return False, field_count, "language-fields"
    if not offsets_preserved:
        return False, len(normalized), "citation-offsets"
    tokens = tuple(part for part in normalized.split() if part)
    if not tokens:
        return False, 0, "tokenization"
    assert "v2" in tokens
    return True, len(tokens), "unicode-field-model"
